findings.title: SQL string concatenation
findings.description: SQL query is built via string concatenation or interpolation. Untrusted input concatenated into SQL is the primary vector for SQL injection.
findings.snippet: query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = " + str(user_id)

remediation.why: Concatenated SQL allows attacker-controlled input to alter the query structure and exfiltrate or corrupt data.
remediation.how: Use parameterised queries / prepared statements. Pass user input as bound parameters, not as parts of the SQL string.

findings.title: SQL string concatenation

findings.snippet: cur.execute("DELETE FROM sessions WHERE id = " + sid)

remediation.why: Concatenated SQL allows attacker-controlled input to alter the query structure and exfiltrate or corrupt data.
remediation.how: Use parameterised queries / prepared statements. Pass user input as bound parameters, not as parts of the SQL string.
